2604.13010.title: Lightning OPD: Efficient Post-Training for Large Reasoning Models with Offline On-Policy Distillation
2604.13010.paper_summary: On-policy distillation (OPD) has emerged as an efficient post-training paradigm for large language models. However, standard OPD requires a live teacher inference server throughout training, resulting in substantial infrastructure overhead. In this work, we investigate whether on-policy distillation can be performed offline. A natural approach is to precompute teacher log-probabilities once over SFT rollouts and reuse them during training. In practice, however, this offline variant fails to reliably match the performance of standard OPD. To understand this discrepancy, we identify a previously overlooked condition that is critical for any OPD pipeline, which we term teacher consistency. This condition requires that the same teacher model be used for both supervised fine-tuning and OPD. We show that violating teacher consistency introduces an irreducible gradient bias, causing both offline and online OPD to converge to a suboptimal fixed point regardless of training duration. Building on this insight, we propose Lightning OPD, an offline on-policy distillation framework that enforces teacher consistency by precomputing teacher log-probabilities over SFT rollouts. This design eliminates the need for a live teacher server entirely. We further show that, under teacher consistency, Lightning OPD shares the same optimum as standard OPD, with bounded gradient discrepancy and an implicit regularization effect that helps prevent policy drift. Extensive experiments on mathematical reasoning and code generation demonstrate that Lightning OPD achieves state-of-the-art performance with significantly improved efficiency. Starting from an SFT-initialized Qwen3-8B-Base model, Lightning OPD reaches 69.9% on AIME 2024 in just 30 GPU hours, achieving a 4.0x speedup over standard OPD and substantially lowering the barrier to entry for academic research on LLM post-training.
2604.13010.paper_summary_zh: 在政策蒸餾（OPD）已經成為大型語言模型的一種高效後訓練範式。<lb/>然而，標準的 OPD 在訓練過程中需要一個實時的教師推斷伺服器，這導致了相當大的基礎設施開銷。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們調查了是否可以離線執行政策蒸餾。<lb/>一種自然的方法是對 SFT 展開進行一次性預計算教師的對數概率，並在訓練過程中重複使用它們。<lb/>然而，在實踐中，這種離線變體無法可靠地匹配標準 OPD 的性能。<lb/>為了理解這種差異，我們確定了一個先前被忽視的條件，這對於任何 OPD 流程都是至關重要的，我們稱之為教師一致性。<lb/>這一條件要求在監督微調和 OPD 之間使用相同的教師模型。<lb/>我們顯示，違反教師一致性會引入不可減少的梯度偏差，導致無論訓練持續時間如何，離線和在線 OPD 都會收斂到次優的固定點。<lb/>基於這一見解，我們提出了 Lightning OPD，一種離線的政策蒸餾框架，通過對 SFT 展開預計算教師的對數概率來強制執行教師一致性。<lb/>這一設計完全消除了對實時教師伺服器的需求。<lb/>我們進一步顯示，在教師一致性下，Lightning OPD 與標準 OPD 共享相同的最優解，具有有限的梯度差異和隱式正則化效果，有助於防止策略漂移。<lb/>在數學推理和代碼生成方面的廣泛實驗表明，Lightning OPD 以顯著提高的效率達到了最先進的性能。<lb/>從 SFT 初始化的 Qwen3-8B-Base 模型開始，Lightning OPD 在僅僅 30 個 GPU 小時內在 AIME 2024 上達到了 69.9%，相較於標準 OPD 實現了 4.0 倍的加速，並大幅降低了學術研究 LLM 後訓練的入門門檻。
2604.13010.author: Yecheng Wu et.al.
2604.13010.authors: Yecheng Wu, Song Han, Hai Cai
